one_line_definition: Transient peak surface temperature rise at a moving sliding contact, superimposed on bulk temperature
Material property sqrt(k·rho·c) governing how much heat a surface absorbs from a moving source
Fraction of total frictional/grinding heat conducted into each contacting body
Sudden surface damage on lubricated rubbing components caused by breakdown of the oil film at high flash temperatures
Highest temperature elevation at the workpiece surface during one grinding pass
In-process temperature sensor formed by a thin metallic foil ground into the workpiece surface, acting as a thermocouple junction
Fractional error introduced by a sensor that perturbs the thermal field it is measuring
Length of the grinding wheel–workpiece contact zone, governing heat source footprint

Material removal using hard abrasive particles at high speed, capable of competing with conventional cutting
Standard technique for measuring thermal diffusivity by pulsed laser heating of one face and recording rear-face temperature rise
Analytical solution for laser flash data including facial and radial heat losses from a cylindrical sample
Ratio of thermal conductivity to volumetric heat capacity, governing rate of temperature diffusion
Statistical description of surface topography deviations from a mean plane
Third-moment statistical parameter describing asymmetry of surface height distribution; negative for honed/ground surfaces
Fourth-moment parameter measuring peakedness of the height distribution
Flexible family of probability distributions that generalizes Gaussian to describe non-Gaussian surface height PDFs
Fourier-transform of the height PDF used in Kirchhoff scattering theory to relate reflection coefficient to surface statistics
Ultrasonic measurement setup where transmitter and receiver are at oblique angles to the surface; used to characterize Rq
Ultrasonic measurement setup where transmitter and receiver are co-located normal to the surface; used to characterize Rsk
Dimensionless number V_w·l_e/alpha_w characterizing the ratio of advection to diffusion in the workpiece during grinding
Analytical framework for temperature fields generated by a heat source moving along a solid surface
